extraction.one_line_summary: GANNA won the opening time trial by 22 seconds over ALMEIDA, while THOMAS established 32-second gap over main GC rivals including YATES.
tactical_patterns: ALMEIDA held hot seat through most GC contenders' finishes, suggesting Portuguese climbing specialists can excel in short technical TTs.
THOMAS gained decisive 32-second advantage over YATES on opening 15km TT, revealing unusual early-Giro form disparity between co-leaders.
Technical crash at 6km from finish shows late-course hazard section severe enough to end GC campaigns before mountains.

rider_signals.GANNA Filippo: positive: delivered expected win by 22 seconds as world TT champion, confirming pre-race favoritism
rider_signals.ALMEIDA João: positive: held hot seat through most GC field as climbing specialist, revealing unexpected TT strength
rider_signals.THOMAS Geraint: positive: gained 32 seconds on co-leader YATES and dominated GC group, showing peak early condition
rider_signals.YATES Simon: neutral: 13:27 behind winner but only 32 seconds down on THOMAS reveals narrow GC spread despite large time gaps
rider_signals.FUGLSANG Jakob: negative: poor time trial combined with team leader crash disaster signals compromised Astana campaign start
rider_signals.NIBALI Vincenzo: neutral: middling GC result on home Sicily roads suggests conservative pacing rather than peak form
